data.title: NetEase's Profit Growth Accelerates on Game Strength
data.description:
By Sherry Qin


Chinese videogame giant NetEase reported stronger quarterly profit growth, driven by the strong performance of established titles during the summer break, indicating a clear recovery momentum in its key game business.

The company behind popular games such as "Fantasy Westward Journey" and "Marvel Rivals" on Thursday reported a 32% jump in net profit to 8.62 billion yuan, equivalent to $1.21 billion. Analysts had expected 8.825 billion yuan, according to a FactSet consensus estimate. Adjusted net profit, a closely watched metric that excludes share-based compensation expenses, rose 27% from a year earlier, slightly ahead of estimates.

Revenue at the Hangzhou-based company climbed 8.2% to 28.36 billion yuan, missing market expectations slightly. Sales from games and value-added services rose to 23.3 billion yuan, driven by self-developed games such as "Eggy Party" and "Where Winds Meet."

The solid third-quarter performance comes as NetEase continues to capitalize on its established games, rolling out regular updates and expansions and strengthening operations. More than 20 years after its launch, the original PC version of "Fantasy Westward Journey" broke its own concurrent-user record earlier this month, surpassing 3.58 million gamers after the game maker launched a new server in the summer.

NetEase's robust game pipeline is expected to continue supporting its top line. Citi analysts highlighted that it has two more game launches in the typically soft fourth quarter, following four launches in October.

The game maker's next most-anticipated title in 2026 is "Ananta," a free-to-play role-playing game. It received an overwhelming response from the gaming community following the live demo conducted in September, the analysts said in a note.

NetEase's Hong Kong-listed shares have rebounded by more than 50% this year, bolstered by improved market sentiment toward the videogame industry amid a more stable regulatory environment. Investors have also rewarded the company for its diverse and expanding game portfolio, which has boosted user engagement and spending per user despite macroeconomic uncertainties.

Last week, NetEase's larger rival Tencent reported resilient results, with its international games revenue surging 43% in the third quarter.


Write to Sherry Qin at sherry.qin@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-20-25 0431ET

Bayer Receives Accelerated U.S. Approval for Lung Cancer Drug
data.description:
BERLIN (dpa-AFX) - The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has granted Bayer accelerated approval for a lung cancer medication. Hyrnuo, which contains the active ingredient Sevabertinib, has been approved for the treatment of previously treated patients with advanced non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) who have been found to carry mutations in the HER2 protein, the pharmaceutical and agrochemical group announced on Thursday.

According to the company, the accelerated approval is based on the objective response rate (ORR) and duration of response (DOR) observed in the ongoing Phase I/II SOHO-01 study. In this trial, the drug was administered to patients with advanced NSCLC and a HER2-activating mutation, whose disease continued to progress despite at least one systemic therapy.

Globally, it is estimated that up to 84,000 people are diagnosed each year with HER2-mutated NSCLC, the statement continued. According to Bayer, these patients generally have a poor prognosis and limited treatment options. "The FDA approval of Sevabertinib opens up a new therapeutic option," study lead Xiuning Le was quoted as saying in the release.

Whether Bayer will also receive permanent approval for Sevabertinib for this indication will likely depend on the review and demonstration of clinical benefit in a confirmatory study, the company added. Such trials are typically required following an accelerated approval./mis/tav/nas

data.description:
BERLIN (Reuters) -German union Verdi said on Thursday it has agreed with Lufthansa's Eurowings business on salary increases and other bonuses for 1,800 flight attendants.

Verdi said Eurowings' cabin crew had agree to a pay increase of a total of 5.4 per cent in two stages.

(Writing by Madeline ChambersEditing by Ludwig Burger)

data.title: Nvidia Will Lose Market Share: Gabelli's John Belton
data.description:

He tells Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld on "The Close" that demand continues to outstrip supply, which is why Nvidia's revenue has grown so steadily in recent quarters.

AstraZeneca gets US approval for Koselugo for rare genetic disorder
data.description:
(Alliance News) - AstraZeneca PLC on Thursday said US regulators have approved its drug Koselugo for use in adults with neurofibromatosis type 1, expanding access to a treatment already used in children with the rare genetic condition.

The Cambridge, England-based pharmaceutical company said the US Food & Drug Administration granted approval based on results from Komet, the first and only placebo-controlled phase 3 trial in adults with NF1 and inoperable plexiform neurofibromas.

Neurofibromatosis type 1 is a lifelong, progressive condition caused by mutations in the NF1 gene. Up to half of patients develop plexiform neurofibromas, which are non-cancerous tumours that can grow along nerves and become debilitating.

The study showed a 20% overall response rate in tumour shrinkage for patients treated with Koselugo, compared with 5% for those on placebo.

AstraZeneca said 86% of responders maintained tumour reduction for at least six months. Safety results were consistent with the medicines established profile in paediatric use.

Marc Dunoyer, chief executive officer of Alexion, AstraZenecas rare disease unit, said the approval "enables much-needed continuity of care" for patients as they age, following the recent US approval of a granule formulation for children aged one and above.

Koselugo for adults has also recently secured approval in the EU and Japan, with additional regulatory reviews ongoing.

AstraZeneca shares were up 1.2% at 13,762.00 pence in London on Thursday morning.

By Eva Castanedo, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to newsroom@alliancenews.com

data.title: Halma plc Reports Earnings Results for the Half Year Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Halma plc reported earnings results for the half year ended September 30, 2025. For the half year, the company reported sales was GBP 1,237.4 million compared to GBP 1,074.3 million a year ago. Net income was GBP 186.8 million compared to GBP 136.2 million a year ago.

Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was GBP 0.4944 compared to GBP 0.3608 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was GBP 0.4929 compared to GBP 0.3598 a year ago.

data.title: Auterion says Rheinmetall stake to boost drone software group's growth
data.description:
DUESSELDORF, Germany (Reuters) -Auterion CEO Lorenz Meier said having German defence player Rheinmetall on board as a "significant" shareholder in the U.S.-based drone software firm would help it grow its order book, adding that it would not lead to a full takeover.

Auterion already provides software for some Rheinmetall drones and the direct investment, which was announced this week, is a way to strengthen collaboration, Meier told Reuters.

He did not disclose the exact size of Rheinmetall's stake, which while "significant", was not a blocking minority.

"We're incredibly pleased with this honour, especially in the German market," said Meier, adding that closer ties between the two firms could help Auterion win more contracts.

STRONG COLLABORATION, NOT TAKEOVER

Rheinmetall CEO Armin Papperger told analysts on Tuesday that the defence firm had bought Auterion shares, without giving any additional details. 

Meier said Rheinmetall, which sees mergers and acquisitions as key to rapidly expanding its business to meet demand, is not aiming for a takeover with the move and Auterion is capable of remaining independent for another 30 years.

Auterion is currently exploring the market for more software functions to acquire, he added.

ORDER BOOKS EXPECTED TO KEEP GROWING

Rheinmetall and Auterion, which signed a long-term cooperation agreement in December 2024, are working together on drone components with the goal of creating an industry standard for controlling and operating them in various environments.

Auterion was founded in 2018 and became profitable this year. It has annual revenue of around 100 million euros ($115 million) and 160 employees, both of which Meier expects to grow.

Meier said Auterion is providing software for the drones built by Rheinmetall to fulfil orders for loitering munitions, which hover over targets before striking. 

Auterion, whose customers already include the U.S. and Ukraine, as well as the Netherlands, Britain and Taiwan, expects to win an order from Germany after demonstrating a drone swarm control system three months ago, Meier said.    

Rheinmetall plans to restructure from next year to reflect changed priorities, including a new air defence unit with mid-term sales potential of 3 billion euros to 4 billion euros.

Its CEO Papperger said demand is mainly being driven by European interest in the face of repeated drone incursions.

($1 = 0.8680 euros)

(Reporting by Matthias Inverardi and Miranda Murray; Editing by Alexander Smith)

By Matthias Inverardi and Miranda Murray

Orlen S.A. Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Orlen S.A. reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported sales was PLN 61,007 million compared to PLN 67,936 million a year ago. Net income was PLN 2,135 million compared to PLN 222 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was PLN 1.84 compared to PLN 0.19 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was PLN 1.84 compared to PLN 0.19 a year ago.
For the nine months, sales was PLN 195,201 million compared to PLN 219,778 million a year ago. Net income was PLN 7,981 million compared to PLN 3,020 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was PLN 6.87 compared to PLN 2.6 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was PLN 6.87 compared to PLN 2.6 a year ago.

data.title: Hong Kong's Inflation Edges Up to 1.2% in October
Hong Kong Stocks End Little Changed as AI-Bubble Fears Cool
U.S. Futures, European Stocks Rise
data.description:
U.S. futures for the S&P 500 were up 1.3% and futures for the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.6%. Changes in futures do not necessarily predict movements after the opening bell.

In Europe, the Stoxx Europe 600 gained 0.9% in morning trading. Halma PLC climbed 9.4% and Games Workshop Group added 8.6%. On the other hand, Allegro.eu lost 5.6%, and Valeo fell 5.2%. The FTSE 100 added 0.7%. Other stocks in Europe were up as France's CAC 40 increased 1.1% and Germany's DAX rose 1%.

The Wall Street Journal Dollar Index climbed 0.1% to 97.58.

In commodities, Brent crude rose 0.6% to $63.89 a barrel, and WTI crude rose 0.7% to $59.64 a barrel. The European benchmark price for natural gas, the Dutch futures contract TTF, was up 0.2% to 31.01 euros a megawatt hour.

The German 10-year Bund yield rose by 1 basis point to 2.722% from 2.717%, and the yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury declined by 1 basis point to 4.135% from 4.142%. Bond prices and yields move in opposite directions.

Stocks in Asia were mixed as Japan's Nikkei 225 index climbed 2.7%, whereas Hong Kong's Hang Seng was flat. China's benchmark Shanghai Composite declined 0.4%.


-This article was automatically generated by MarketWatch.


Write to Barcelona Editors at barcelonaeditors@dowjones.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

data.title: Japan nuclear watchdog says confidential documents mishandled at Kashiwazaki-Kariwa plant
Indonesia finance minister says invitation from central bank to attend rate meeting will help achieve policy synergy
European Markets Rise After Tech Earnings Beat Expectations
data.description:
(Alliance News) - On Thursday, major European stock indices opened in positive territory on a relatively quiet day for macroeconomic data, buoyed by Nvidia's earnings, which exceeded expectations and eased fears of a tech bubble that had previously rattled markets.

The FTSE Mib was up 1.2% at 43,178.52, the Mid-Cap gained 0.9% to 57,638.86, the Small-Cap climbed 0.8% to 35,035.29, and the Italy Growth index edged up 0.1% to 8,350.29.

London's FTSE 100 was up 0.7%, Paris's CAC 40 rose 1.1%, and Frankfurt's DAX 40 advanced 0.9%.

On the macroeconomic front, Germany's producer prices fell by 1.8% year-on-year in October, following a 1.7% decline in September and compared to market expectations of a 1.9% drop.

This marks the eighth consecutive month of annual producer price declines. The overall decrease was mainly driven by a 7.5% drop in energy costs.

In the United States, Federal Reserve officials were divided during the October Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting, with "many" suggesting it would be prudent to keep interest rates unchanged for the rest of the year.

Although the FOMC approved a quarter-point rate cut during the October meeting, minutes revealed that several officials opposed lowering the target range, while some supported the decision but could have also backed maintaining the status quo.

Additionally, the minutes showed that participants expressed "strongly divergent views" on the most appropriate policy decision for the December meeting.

Back in Milan, at Piazza Affari, only three stocks were in negative territory: Stellantis was at the bottom, down 0.3%. Campari also declined, losing 0.2%, while DiaSorin slipped 0.1%. DiaSorin and Qiagen launched the new Liaison QuantiFeron-Tb Gold Plus II test, CE marked, which increases hourly analysis capacity by 75% and reduces response times for tuberculosis diagnosis by 25%.

Prysmian led the pack, rising 2.6%.

Banks also performed well, with BPM up 2.2% and Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena rounding out the top three with a 2.1% gain.

STMicroelectronics advanced 0.7%. The company and TSE, a leader in solar energy and agrivoltaics in France, announced Thursday that they have signed a physical power purchase agreement for the supply of renewable electricity from solar parks to STM sites in France.

TSE will supply STMicroelectronics with renewable energy generated by three solar parks in France, managed by TSE, totaling approximately 43 MW. This 15-year contract, starting in 2027, represents a total volume of about 780 GWh.

Leonardo - up 0.7% - announced Wednesday, in partnership with EDGE Group, the establishment of general principles for a new joint venture in the United Arab Emirates, expected in 2026. The focus will be on sensors, systems integration, and defense platforms.

On the Mid-Cap, MFE-MediaForEurope announced Thursday it closed the first nine months with a net profit of EUR243.1 million, up from EUR96.2 million as of September 30, 2024.

Revenues for the nine months stood at EUR1.94 billion, down from EUR2.0 billion in the same period of 2024, while advertising revenues were EUR1.92 billion compared to EUR1.94 billion last year.

Ebit as of September 30 was EUR61.3 million, down from EUR126.6 million in the first nine months of 2024. Class A shares rose 3.0%, while Class B shares gained 1.4%.

CIR jumped 7.0%. The company announced that, together with F2i, it reached a binding agreement for CIR to purchase F2i Healthcare's 40.2% stake in KOS, with F2i Healthcare being 61.4% controlled by F2i.

The consideration for the acquired shares amounts to EUR220.0 million.

On the Small-Cap, Alantra maintained its view on Eurotech - up 1.7% - following the quarterly report. Analysts noted that the strengthening of order intake, cost discipline, and a stable financial position set the stage for a stronger year-end, as the market awaits a new strategic plan, seen as the main catalyst for a potential re-rating.

EPH was flat at EUR0.55 per share. Since the board approved RONA's binding offer, the stock has gained 240% in 30 days.

Banca Sistema - down 0.1% - received a payment of EUR103 million from the municipality, the final recipient of a ruling by the European Court of Human Rights, which has since exited financial distress.

Among SMEs, ESPE climbed 2.7%. The company announced Thursday it signed contracts with two new Italian clients for the construction of two advanced agrivoltaic plants: one in Bologna province (9.8 MWp, valued at EUR6.0 million) and one in Treviso province (5.2 MWp, valued at EUR4.5 million).

ESPE Group's total order book amounts to approximately EUR94.3 million, with 95% attributable to the photovoltaic sector.

TMP Group - not yet traded - launched a training project commissioned by Learn International for students at the University of Florida, focusing on communication, media, and sports marketing. The stock closed flat at EUR6.50.

Circle rose 1.2% after announcing Thursday the launch of a strategic project with a leading Mediterranean group active in terminal and shipping services, aimed at digitally transforming its logistics processes in line with European standards and the objectives of the PNRR.

The project is valued at nearly EUR300,000.

In Asia, the Nikkei closed up 2.7% at 49,823.94, the Shanghai Composite ended down 0.4% at 3,931.05, and the Hang Seng closed marginally higher at 25,835.57.

In New York, at Wednesday's close, the Dow gained 0.1% to 46,138.77, the Nasdaq rose 0.6% to 22,564.23, and the S&P 500 advanced 0.4% to 6,642.16.

In currency markets, the euro was trading at USD1.1517, down from USD1.1536 at Wednesday's equity close, while the pound was at USD1.3067 from USD1.3076 the previous evening.

Among commodities, Brent was trading at USD63.94 a barrel, up from USD63.35 at Wednesday's close, while gold was at USD4,051.75 an ounce, down from USD4,075.54 an ounce Wednesday evening.

On Thursday's economic calendar, construction production data for the Eurozone is expected at 1100 CET.

Spanish consumer confidence will be released at 1300 CET, while in the afternoon, focus will shift to U.S. data on unemployment, housing, exports, and wages.

Eurozone consumer confidence will be published at 1600 CET.

At Piazza Affari, Piquadro, Laboratorio Farmaceutico Erfo, and iVision Tech will release their results.

By Claudia Cavaliere, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2025 Alliance News IS Italian Service Ltd. All rights reserved.

data.title: AB Sagax to Reposition Nokia's Facilities in Oulu, Finland
Resilient domestic economy gives India space to negotiate U.S. trade deal, sources say
data.description:
NEW DELHI/MUMBAI (Reuters) -India's strong domestic economy and a smaller-than-expected hit to exports are giving New Delhi more room to negotiate a trade deal with Washington, which has imposed tariffs as high as 50% on imports from the country, Indian sources and analysts said.

Indian exports to the U.S. dropped 8.6% year-on-year to $6.3 billion in October, the second month that the 50% tariff on imports from the country was in place. The decline was lesser than the 12% drop in September.

Negotiations between New Delhi and Washington have dragged on even as other Asian countries like Japan and South Korea have struck deals to bring down tariffs. Indian officials have publicly maintained that India will not rush to sign a deal.

"For now, we've avoided the worst impact of the 50% U.S. tariffs," said a senior government official aware of the talks, declining to be identified as the person was not authorised to speak to the media.

While some sectors such as textiles have reported a fall in U.S. orders, the official said, the broader economic impact of the tariffs has been limited, allowing negotiators space to reach a deal.

"If needed, we are ready to wait," the official said.

The official, and others aware of the trade talks, said they expect Washington to roll back the 25% tariff linked to Russian oil purchases, and eventually move toward a 15% overall rate, with New Delhi prepared to cut its own import tariffs on over 80% of goods while protecting sensitive sectors like agriculture.

The commerce ministry did not immediately respond to an emailed request for comment. U.S. officials did not immediately comment although President Donald Trump said last week Washington was close to reaching a deal with India that would expand economic and security ties.

The government is helping exporters diversify into new markets through recent new trade deals with the U.K., UAE and Australia, tax cuts on raw materials and a $5.1 billion support package to boost exporters, officials said.

Many exporters have cushioned the fall in exports to the U.S. since September by diversifying into African and European markets and retaining U.S. clients with discounts and longer delivery timelines, five exporters and industry associations said.

Apparel and footwear firms are absorbing costs up to 20% to retain U.S. buyers, said Ajay Sahai, director general of the Federation of Indian Export Organisations.

The government and the central bank have announced targeted relief, including short-term loan moratoriums, but avoided large fiscal easing.

Domestic tax cuts on hundreds of consumer items since September are boosting local demand and helping exporters stay competitive, trade associations and officials say.

Also, tax cuts on inputs like man-made fibres have aided textile exporters, said N Thirukkumaran, general secretary of the Tirupur Exporters' Association.

Exporters are offering 10-20% discounts on garments depending on style and shipment size, he said.

India's economy grew an annual 7% in the July-September quarter and is expected to expand 6.8% in the financial year, according to the central bank.

CHINA COMPETITION INTENSIFIES

However, exporters said that pressure from over-capacity in China is proving to be tough to manage, with cheaper Chinese goods flooding a number of the markets they compete in.

"Chinese businesses are well-entrenched and their domestic situation has made them highly competitive," said Rahul Tikoo, CEO of Optime, a Mumbai-based speciality chemical maker.

India's goods exports to non-U.S. markets fell 12.5% year-on-year in October, a sharper drop than to the U.S, led by weaker engineering, petroleum and jewellery shipments.

"This may reflect heightened competition in non-U.S. markets as countries diversify exports post tariff announcements," HSBC chief India economist Pranjul Bhandari said.

(Reporting by Ashwin Manikandan in Mumbai and Manoj Kumar in New Delhi; Editing by Raju Gopalakrishnan)

By Manoj Kumar and Ashwin Manikandan
